Philosophy: The economy of Light and Shadow is a fully player-driven, closed-loop value flow ecosystem. Value is sustained through high-friction gameplay loops, steep death costs, non-inflationary pacing, and unrestricted trade liquidity. It aims to prevent hyper-inflation, preserve mid-tier usefulness, and balance high-risk activities with long-term capital safety.

CanonicalRules.Text: The currency supply must remain in a balanced state where gold faucets (monster drops, quests, world events) are strictly counterbalanced by structural sinks (blessings, market taxes, guild registration).

Combat Time-to-Kill (TTK) serves as a physical throttle on currency generation. High TTK and tactical depth prevent automated speed-farming and limit the absolute hourly rate of gold entering the system.

Every death (PvP or PvE) wipes out all active blessings, forcing players to spend up to 1.76M Bronze Coins (176 Gold Coins) to replenish full 100% item protection. This is the primary gold sink in the game.
All asynchronous market transactions carry a flat, non-refundable 2% listing fee, acting as a continuous, high-volume gold sink proportional to economic activity.

All items in the world (including legendary boss drops and crafted gear) are 100% tradable. Soulbinding, character-binding, and bind-on-pickup mechanics are strictly forbidden to maximize trade velocity.

Unprotected deaths (deaths occurred with fewer than 7 active blessings) cause linear item drops to the character's corpse. While dropped items are redistributed via Open Loot, a flat 15% 'Shatter Rate' applies to dropped items, permanently destroying them to act as a systemic gear sink.
Banked gold is 100% safe from all death penalties, encouraging capital security, whereas carried currency is subject to immediate loot loss upon death, reinforcing physical risk in high-danger zones.

Faucets.MonsterDrops: Civilized humanoids (bandits, cultists, pirates) drop direct Bronze, Silver, and raw Gold. Beasts and elementals drop valuable raw crafting components.
Faucets.QuestContracts: Dynamic faction contract completions award progressive tier-based currency based on region difficulty scaling.
Faucets.WorldEvents: Large-scale rifts and continental boss events dispense high-value physical chests with concentrated currency and rare items.
Faucets.SalvageTrade: NPC vendors purchase junk items and surplus materials at baseline floor rates, establishing a minimum gold value for low-tier drops.

Sinks.BlessingReplenishment: 7 Primordial Altars distributed across continents sell blessings. Total replenishment cost for full protection is 1,760,000 Bronze (176 Gold). Consumed entirely on any death.
Sinks.MarketListingFee: A flat 2.0% listing fee is charged upfront upon posting any item to the global market. Non-refundable upon sale cancellation.
Sinks.CraftingSurcharges: Tier 1 to Tier 3 crafting recipes require direct currency payments to regional artisan NPCs alongside raw material ingredients.
Sinks.GuildAndHouseSinks: Guild registration, emblem customization, rank upgrades, and bids on player houses require heavy capital gold investments that are permanently burned from the active economy.

EconomicFlowModel.LiquidityVelocity: Because items never bind, trade velocity is infinite. A Tier 5 sword can circulate through dozens of owners over its lifespan, maintaining high secondary market volume and currency circulation.

EconomyStabilityIndex.Definition: A system-wide economic health score used to diagnose deflationary contraction or inflationary pressure.

TargetRange.InflationAlert: Below 0.80 (Currency is entering faster than it is burned. Triggers increase in Altar blessing costs and artisan fees.)
TargetRange.DeflationAlert: Above 1.20 (Currency is too scarce, players are hoarding. Triggers decrease in listing fees and increased quest contract payouts.)

Stages.Source: Crafting (restricted to T1-T3) or World Drop (T1-T5 exclusively from high-risk dungeons and world bosses).

Stages.Description: Equipped on player character. No durability decay exists, ensuring consistent gameplay performance.
Traded via Direct Secure Trade, physical Handoff Trade, or the Global City-Linked Market.
Taken into high-hazard biomes or PvP conflict areas. Protected by blessings or exposed to death drop risks.

On unprotected death: items are dropped. 85% of dropped items become 'Open Loot' on the corpse for any player to retrieve. 15% of dropped items undergo systemic 'Shattering' and are permanently deleted from the database.

SystemIntegrations.CombatSystem: High TTK limits maximum farming efficiency. Action-based combat prevents automated macros from running infinite farming loops.
SystemIntegrations.WorldFoundation: Dangerous biomes scale encounter density and hazards. Higher-risk zones yield exponentially higher-tier loot drops and raw currency carriers, creating a physical high-risk high-reward frontier.
SystemIntegrations.ProgressionSystem: XP and skill progression are infinite but soft-capped, preventing power explosion. This keeps T3/T4 gear viable even for high-level characters, preventing rapid devaluation of mid-tier items.
